agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Real-time courier tracking feature for customer-facing delivery experience. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements regarding courier location visibility. Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator; Operations demands zero location exposure due to measurable driver attrition. This is a foundational product decision that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver fulfilling an active delivery order. In this context, the human whose real-time location is the subject of the dispute."},{"term":"Real-time Location","definition":"GPS coordinates of the courier, updated at a frequency sufficient to convey live movement (typically every 5-15 seconds)."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The time period from order dispatch to delivery completion, during which tracking would be active."},{"term":"Location Granularity","definition":"The precision at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to approximate zone or ETA-only."},{"term":"Driver Retention","definition":"The ability to keep couriers on the platform. Operations reports that location transparency is a causal factor in driver churn."},{"term":"Customer Transparency","definition":"The degree to which delivery progress is visible to the end customer. Customer Success positions this as a core brand differentiator."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, loading dock wait)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on whether location is shown at all. If shown, stationary dots create customer anxiety ('why isn't my courier moving?'). If not shown, this is moot."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (multi-drop, personal errand)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS exposes deviations that may be operationally valid but appear wrong to customers. Approximate tracking or ETA-based approaches avoid this."},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer's home but serving another order first","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — showing exact location here creates customer frustration ('they're right here, why haven't they delivered?'). This is a known pain point in multi-drop logistics."},{"scenario":"Courier ends shift or goes off-duty mid-route","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — if GPS is always shown, the transition from active to reassigned must be handled. Privacy implications extend to off-duty periods."},{"scenario":"Courier in a sensitive or private location (hospital, domestic violence shelter)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS of couriers between deliveries could expose locations that create safety risks for the courier or third parties."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk in both directions. Losing driver supply (Operations concern) directly constrains delivery capacity and increases recruitment/onboarding costs — typically $500-2000 per driver. Losing customer differentiation (Customer Success concern) impacts conversion and retention, but the magnitude is unquantified. Neither team has presented ROI data.","operational":"Driver churn is an immediate, measurable operational crisis — fewer drivers means longer delivery times, more failed deliveries, and cascading SLA violations. Customer satisfaction is important but does not break operations in the same acute way.","user":"Two user groups in direct tension: end customers want visibility and confidence their delivery is coming; couriers want autonomy and freedom from surveillance. Both are 'users' of the platform.","risk":"CRITICAL: Building either requirement as specified creates business harm. Full GPS satisfies customers but accelerates driver attrition. Zero visibility satisfies drivers but removes a feature customers increasingly expect as table stakes in 2026. Legal/privacy risk also unassessed — GDPR, labor law, and local privacy regulations may constrain what is even permissible.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery experience and trust in the platform depends on some form of visibility. Exact GPS vs. approximate vs. ETA-only creates very different experiences."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their sense of autonomy, workplace dignity, and ultimately their decision to remain on the platform. This is a labor relations issue, not just a feature toggle."},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"Their KPIs (NPS, CSAT, churn) depend on transparency features. Need to understand whether exact GPS is truly the driver of satisfaction or whether approximate tracking achieves the same outcome."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Their KPIs (driver retention, fulfillment rate, delivery capacity) are directly threatened by surveillance-perceived features. They own the supply side of the marketplace."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"NOT YET CONSULTED. Real-time GPS tracking of workers has regulatory implications in multiple jurisdictions (GDPR Article 6/9, California CCPA, EU labor directives). This stakeholder is missing from the conversation entirely."},{"group":"Executive Leadership","impact":"This is a strategic product direction decision that trades off supply-side health against demand-side experience. Only executive authority can resolve this tradeoff."}]},"confidence":{"level":30,"reasoning":"Confidence is 30 because the specification contains a hard contradiction between two authoritative stakeholder teams, both claiming non-negotiable status. Additionally, a critical stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) has not been consulted, and there is no data provided to quantify either side's claims. The Customer Success team has not proven that exact GPS (vs. approximate) drives their metrics. The Operations team has not provided attrition data with controls. We cannot define correct behavior when the definition of correct is itself in dispute.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine contradiction that cannot be resolved by engineering","Both requirements as stated are mutually exclusive","There are well-established industry patterns (approximate tracking, ETA-based) that may satisfy both parties partially","Legal/privacy review is mandatory before any implementation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether exact GPS is truly the customer differentiator or whether approximate tracking achieves the same satisfaction","Whether driver attrition is caused by GPS tracking specifically or by broader surveillance culture","What jurisdictional privacy constraints apply to this specific deployment","Whether couriers would accept opt-in or configurable visibility as a compromise"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and representing real business concerns","No executive decision has already been made that supersedes either team","The platform operates in jurisdictions where worker location tracking has regulatory implications","There exist intermediate solutions between 'exact GPS always' and 'no location ever'"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These requirements are mutually exclusive as stated. Both teams claim non-negotiable status. This is not a technical problem — it is a business strategy conflict that requires executive arbitration.","affected_stakeholders":["End Customers","Couriers","Customer Success Team","Operations Team","Legal/Compliance","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Option A: Full GPS transparency (Customer Success wins, Operations loses — expect continued driver attrition)","Option B: No location visibility (Operations wins, Customer Success loses — competitive disadvantage vs. every major delivery platform)","Option C: Approximate location only — show courier within a radius (e.g., 200m-500m) or on a simplified route, not exact coordinates. Balances transparency with privacy.","Option D: ETA-based tracking only — show estimated arrival time updated in real-time, no map pin. Satisfies 'where is my delivery' without exposing courier location.","Option E: Courier opt-in — let couriers choose whether to share location, with incentives for those who do. Respects autonomy while enabling the feature.","Option F: Progressive disclosure — show approximate zone when courier is far, more detail only in final delivery mile. Time-bounded precision."],"recommendation":"Option C (approximate location) or Option F (progressive disclosure) are the industry-standard resolution. They satisfy the customer's actual need ('is my delivery coming and roughly when') without creating the surveillance dynamic that drives attrition. However, this is an EXECUTIVE DECISION — I recommend convening Customer Success, Operations, Legal, and a product executive to review driver attrition data, customer satisfaction drivers, and regulatory constraints before any implementation begins. Do not build anything until this is resolved."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and Compliance have not been consulted. Real-time GPS tracking of workers is regulated under GDPR (legitimate interest balancing, DPIA required), various US state privacy laws, and labor regulations in multiple jurisdictions. Building either version without legal review creates compliance risk.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance for a privacy impact assessment before any implementation","Defer feature until regulatory requirements are mapped per operating jurisdiction"],"recommendation":"Mandatory legal review before any acceptance criteria are defined. In some jurisdictions, the Operations team's position may be legally required regardless of business preference."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot accept Customer Success requirement as stated","business_rule_broken":"Exact GPS coordinate exposure directly contradicts Operations team's non-negotiable requirement and creates unquantified driver retention risk and potential privacy law violations."},{"violation":"Cannot accept Operations requirement as stated","business_rule_broken":"Zero location visibility contradicts Customer Success team's non-negotiable requirement and removes a feature that is industry-standard in courier delivery, creating competitive disadvantage."},{"violation":"No implementation should proceed under contradictory requirements","business_rule_broken":"Building to either spec without executive resolution will result in rework, stakeholder conflict, and potential regulatory exposure. Engineering must not be asked to resolve a business strategy dispute by picking a side."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Real-time courier tracking feature for customer-facing delivery experience. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements regarding courier location visibility. Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator; Operations demands zero location exposure due to measurable driver attrition.
